sentence_reviews.text: Mechanistic interpretability studies circuits, features, attention patterns, sparse representations, and algorithmic structure inside trained neural networks.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Transformer, Deep Learning, and PML sources support neural networks, attention, features, representations, and sparse coding foundations; mechanistic framing is synthesized.
sentence_reviews.notes: Concept is well supported by dependencies, but packet lacks a dedicated mechanistic-interpretability source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis is to connect Feature attribution & interpretability, Ablation, interventions & causal analysis to downstream capabilities including Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support interpretability, causal/ablation-style analysis, model components, and evaluation governance; observability linkage is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible graph synthesis, not exact source support.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports monitoring, measurement, and evaluation governance but not this exact downstream relationship.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible, but a tracing/observability source would be needed for direct support.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports interpretability and representation analysis foundations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Combination is plausible and aligned with the taxonomy.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Feature attribution & interpretability, Ablation, interventions & causal analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support broad evaluation limits and dependency concepts but not the exact mechanistic-interpretability limitation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Generic limitation should be narrowed to mechanistic-interpretability coverage/scalability limits.

sentence_reviews.text: Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Feature attribution & interpretability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports interpretability and feature/representation analysis as foundations for model analysis.

sentence_reviews.text: Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Ablation, interventions & causal analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML/AIMA support causal and intervention reasoning; ablation-style analysis is a common way to test mechanisms.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core dependency is plausible, though not directly sourced to mechanistic interpretability.

sentence_reviews.text: Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Transformers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Attention Is All You Need defines transformer architecture and attention mechanisms that are common targets of circuit/attention-pattern analysis.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common dependency is well supported for transformer-focused mechanistic work.

sentence_reviews.text: Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Autoencoders.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning covers autoencoders as representation-learning models; sparse/feature methods can use autoencoder-like components.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible for sparse autoencoder methods, but the packet cites only general autoencoder background.

sentence_reviews.text: Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Sparse coding & compositional representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning and PML support sparse coding and distributed/compositional representation concepts.

edge_reviews.notes: Interpretability is a core parent concept for mechanistic interpretability.
Ablations and interventions are central ways to test proposed mechanisms.
Transformers are a common target architecture; common strength is appropriate.
Autoencoders are relevant but the cited general source does not establish this edge directly.
Sparse/distributed representation concepts are a common mechanistic-interpretability substrate.
